summary: Six confirmed issues in ./demo-app: committed secrets, a client-exposed admin token, unauthenticated admin APIs, IDOR on notes, SQL injection in search, and stored XSS in note rendering. Immediate remediation required before any production deploy.
topExploitChain: Read sk-fake keys from committed demo-app/.env → extract window.__ADMIN_TOKEN from the client bundle → call GET /api/admin unauthenticated → enumerate other users' notes via ?userId= → exfiltrate data using SQLi on /api/search → persist XSS payload in a note rendered by Note.tsx for session hijack.
demoScript: Open the audit UI and review grade F with six findings across secrets, auth, and injection.
Walk the top exploit chain: secrets → admin token → open admin API → IDOR → SQLi → stored XSS.
Generate fix PLAN, copy for Cursor, and paste into the demo-app workspace to remediate in one session.

findings.title: Committed .env with live-format API keys and database credentials
findings.exploitScenario: Clone the repo or browse history; attacker harvests keys and connection strings for lateral movement into CI, cloud, and database hosts.
findings.fixHint: Remove .env from git, rotate all exposed values, add .env to .gitignore, and load secrets from a vault or deployment env only.

findings.title: Admin token embedded in client bundle via inline script
findings.exploitScenario: View page source or DevTools; read window.__ADMIN_TOKEN and replay it on privileged routes or admin tooling.
findings.fixHint: Never ship admin tokens to the browser; use httpOnly session cookies and server-only env vars for privileged operations.

findings.title: Admin API exposes user list without authentication
findings.evidence:
// VULN: no authentication on admin endpoint
return NextResponse.json({ users: ["alice", "bob"], role: "admin" });
findings.exploitScenario: curl GET /api/admin — unauthenticated caller receives admin JSON and can invoke DELETE to wipe data.
findings.fixHint: Require session or JWT with admin role on every handler; reject anonymous requests with 401/403.

findings.title: IDOR: notes endpoint returns any user's data by userId param
findings.exploitScenario: Iterate ?userId=1,2,… to read other users' notes without logging in as them.
findings.fixHint: Bind notes to the authenticated principal from the session; ignore client-supplied userId for authorization.

findings.title: SQL injection via unsanitized search query concatenation
findings.evidence:
const q = searchParams.get("q") ?? "";
const query = `SELECT * FROM items WHERE title LIKE '%${q}%'`;
findings.exploitScenario: Request /api/search?q=' OR '1'='1 — crafted input breaks out of the LIKE clause for full table read or destructive statements if executed.
findings.fixHint: Use parameterized queries / an ORM; never interpolate user input into SQL strings.

findings.title: Stored XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML on note content
findings.evidence:
// VULN: renders unsanitized HTML
return <div dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: content }} />;
findings.exploitScenario: Save a note containing ; victims viewing the note execute attacker script.
findings.fixHint: Sanitize HTML with a vetted library, or render plain text only; avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML for user content.
